title: carex nubigena subsp. pseudoarenicola
intro: Carex nubigena subsp. pseudoarenicola, also known as mountain sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of sedge that grows in mountainous regions, and its medicinal properties have been recognized for their potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Carex nubigena subsp. pseudoarenicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, relieve coughs, and support immune function.
